summary: Full-stack browser-based real-time hack-and-slash RPG inspired by Ladder Slasher's gameplay loop. Built with React + TypeScript frontend and Motoko backend on ICP. Features Internet Identity auth, shared persistent on-chain world, ICP-based marketplace, live leaderboard, seasonal resets, hardcore/softcore realms, tick-based combat, class progression with Ascension Trials, and full itemization system.
architectural_notes: Frontend: React + TypeScript with Canvas rendering for dungeon/combat screens
Backend: Motoko canisters on ICP for persistent world state, auth, marketplace, leaderboard, season management
Auth: Internet Identity (passkey-based) tying characters to on-chain accounts
Combat: tick-based (200-500ms) runs client-side; results server-validated on dungeon completion
Modular structure: state/, systems/, ui/, data/ directories for clean separation
Deterministic RNG with seed option for debugging and server-side validation
Season reset triggers automatically when first player completes final class Ascension Trial
Players retain stash/equipment across season resets; class unlock chain resets to Tier 1
Combat ability cycling: per-tick proc roll determines attack vs ability use; ability fully replaces normal attack when triggered
Health is persistent and live: damage carries over between fights; regenerates at +2 HP/sec outside combat; stored on-chain so it survives page refreshes (planned but deferred)
known_issues: Real-time WebSocket co-op (live players in same dungeon) is not supported on ICP platform; multiplayer is shared-world async only
Equipment stats (weapon base damage, armor base defense, affixes) not applied to character effective stats in combat or character sheet
Starter gear not appearing in equipment slots after character creation
Stat allocation during character creation not persisted — all stats revert to 1 after creation
Delete character button missing from character creation flow (only spec'd for character select screen)
Ability points mismatch: character summary shows 1 available point at level 1 but abilities screen shows 0
Character select screen shows 20 HP instead of correct max HP; regen initializes from 20 instead of persisted currentHp
App stuck on loading screen after login — character fetch (getCharacters) hangs indefinitely with no fallback; loading state never clears on error
XP not persisting after dungeon runs — characters return to same level/XP after every fight; combat result submission does not write updated XP/level to stable storage
HP resets to 20 after dungeon runs instead of persisting post-combat HP; regen reinitializes from 20 rather than the actual remaining HP

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication. Users must log in before creating or accessing characters. All on-chain state (characters, inventory, marketplace listings) must be scoped to the authenticated principal.

Implement character creation with two realm choices: Softcore and Hardcore. Each player may have one active character per realm per season. Characters begin at Class Tier 1 (original name: e.g., 'Wanderer'), Level 1, with base stat points to allocate across Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality. Show original in-game descriptions for each stat.

Implement a tick-based combat engine (200–500ms ticks) running client-side. On dungeon completion, submit the combat result to the backend for validation and reward distribution (XP, loot). Combat involves the player and randomly generated monsters; monsters use physical or magical attacks without revealing attack type every round.
Implement 10 original monster types with unique stat profiles (HP, attack, defense, attack speed, loot tables). Monsters are randomly selected and scaled to the current dungeon level.

Implement three dungeon modes: Catacombs (standard pace, full loot to player), Depths (faster pace, higher risk/reward, surprise encounters), and Ascension Trial (the Master Quest gating class tier unlocks). Each mode is selectable from a dungeon selection screen.

Implement leveling and stat allocation. Characters gain XP from combat. On level-up, the player receives stat points to allocate across Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality. Display current stats and their derived effects (damage, speed, HP, defense) on the character sheet.

Implement 8–12 original abilities across melee, ranged, magic, and tank archetypes. Characters may equip up to 3 abilities at a time. Ability points are earned (1 point at start, +1 per 5 levels). Equipped abilities reset when the character rerolls or completes an Ascension Trial.

Implement a loot system with 4 rarity tiers (original names, e.g., Common / Uncommon / Rare / Legendary), randomized affixes (+stats, regen, damage, defense, crit), and item types (weapons, armor, charms/trinkets). Loot drops at dungeon run completion based on monster loot tables and dungeon mode.

Implement an inventory screen with item compare tooltips. Players can equip items to character slots, move items to/from the stash/vault, and view item stats side-by-side with currently equipped items.

Implement an ICP-based marketplace where players can list items for sale at a fixed price or auction, browse listings, and buy items. Cross-player item transfers must be secure and atomic on-chain. Include a safe item vault for holding items intended for transfer.

Implement a live leaderboard showing top players per realm (Softcore and Hardcore), ranked by class tier then level then XP. Hardcore characters who die remain on the current season's leaderboard until the season resets. The leaderboard wipes completely on season reset.

Implement Softcore and Hardcore death mechanics. On Softcore death: apply a penalty (gold loss and/or XP loss) and return the player to the dungeon select screen. On Hardcore death: mark the character as dead permanently, preventing further dungeon runs, but retain the character's stash and keep the character on the current season's leaderboard.

Build all required UI screens: (1) Login/Home screen with Internet Identity, (2) Character sheet with stats, gear slots, and equipped abilities, (3) Dungeon run screen with combat log and visual HP bars, (4) Inventory + stash screen with item compare, (5) Ladder screen with realm tabs, (6) Ascension Trial / Master Quest screen, (7) Marketplace screen with listings, buy/bid, and item vault. Include stub screens for Professions and Shrines marked as 'Coming Soon'.

Apply a cohesive dark fantasy visual theme across all screens: deep stone/obsidian backgrounds, ember and gold accent colors, sharp gothic typography for headings, clean readable body text, subtle particle or glow effects on legendary items and ability icons. The theme should feel like a hand-crafted underground dungeon crawler, not a generic RPG UI.

Fix stat persistence during character creation: when the player allocates 8 stat points and confirms creation, write the allocated Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality values directly into the character's baseStats record in stable storage. Do not use a temporary UI-only state object that gets discarded. Ensure no post-creation initialization step overwrites the assigned values with defaults.

Fix the ability point exploit and rework the ability system: characters start with exactly 1 ability point; earn +1 ability point every 10 levels (so at level 100 a character has 11 total points); each ability purchase costs 1 ability point; block the purchase UI when availableAbilityPoints == 0; enforce a hard cap of 3 simultaneously equipped abilities (players may own more than 3 but only equip 3); expand the ability pool in abilities.ts to approximately 15 abilities spread across melee, ranged, magic, and tank archetypes.

Rebalance monster difficulty: reduce the base damage of all monsters by approximately 10–15% and reduce HP scaling per dungeon level by approximately 10%. Apply these reductions in the monster templates in monsters.ts and in any scaling logic in combatEngine.ts. Keep encounters challenging but smooth out the early progression curve.

Restrict the random item affix pool in lootGenerator.ts to only the following valid stats: Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance. Remove all other affix types that are currently generated (e.g. any regen, speed, or unrecognized stats). Apply this restriction to all item types: weapons, armor, and charms/trinkets.

Reduce the global item drop rate by 50% in lootGenerator.ts. This applies to all dungeon modes (Catacombs, Depths, Ascension Trial) and all monster types. If drops are determined by a probability roll, halve that probability. If drops are determined by a count, halve the expected count.

Completely remove the gold system from the entire codebase: (a) backend — remove all gold fields from character/player records in main.mo, remove any gold rewards in dungeon result processing, remove gold from death penalty logic; (b) frontend — remove gold display from CharacterSheet, Navigation, and any other screen; remove gold reward lines from combat/loot output; update the Softcore death penalty to apply XP loss only (no gold loss); remove any vendor or store UI that requires gold. Gear must be self-found or traded via the marketplace only.

Fix equipment stats not being applied to the character's effective stats. In lootGenerator.ts, add a base physical damage value to all generated Weapon items (scaled by rarity and item level). Add a base defense value to all generated Armor items. In combatEngine.ts, update the player stat derivation logic to iterate over all equipped items and sum their base values (physical damage for weapons, defense for armor) plus all affixes (Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance) on top of the character's base stats before any combat calculation. Ensure the character sheet display in CharacterSheet.tsx also reads these aggregated effective stats rather than base stats alone.

Add a prefix/suffix name generation system for monsters in monsters.ts and wherever monster names are displayed in combatEngine.ts and DungeonRunScreen.tsx. Define a pool of at least 8 thematic prefixes (e.g. Vile, Rotting, Ancient, Cursed, Feral, Festering, Hungering, Corrupted) and at least 6 thematic suffixes (e.g. Brute, Warden, Scout, Shaman, Ravager, Specter). On each monster spawn, randomly combine a prefix and/or suffix with the base monster type name to produce a unique display name (e.g. 'Cursed Goblin Brute', 'Ancient Troll Shaman'). The base stat profile remains unchanged; only the displayed name varies.

Expose Ticks Between Attacks as a visible stat for both the player and each monster. In combatEngine.ts, ensure the player's attack interval (in ticks) is derivable from effective stats (e.g. based on Dexterity or a fixed base modified by stat scaling). On CharacterSheet.tsx, add a 'Ticks Between Attacks' row in the derived stats section showing the player's current value. In DungeonRunScreen.tsx, display each spawned monster's ticks-between-attacks value alongside its HP bar or in the combat info panel. Both values must be read directly from the same tick-rate logic used by the combat engine to stay accurate.

In App.tsx, after a user logs in and a realm is selected, query the backend for an existing character for that realm before showing any screen. If a character already exists for the selected realm in the current season, skip the CharacterCreationScreen entirely and route the player directly to the character sheet / dungeon select screen. The character creation screen must only be shown when no character exists for the chosen realm.

In App.tsx, if createCharacter is called and returns an #alreadyExists error (e.g., due to a race condition or duplicate call), the frontend must silently redirect the player to the character sheet / dungeon select screen rather than displaying an error message or crashing.

In combatEngine.ts, implement ability cycling on each attack tick. When a character has 1 equipped ability, roll 87.5% normal attack / 12.5% ability. With 2 equipped abilities, roll 75% normal attack / 12.5% each ability. With 3 equipped abilities, roll 62.5% normal attack / 12.5% each ability. When an ability procs, it fully replaces the normal attack with that ability's defined effect (damage multiplier, type, AoE, status, etc.) from abilities.ts. The normal attack only fires when no ability is selected by the roll.

In combatEngine.ts, set the player's base ticks-between-attacks to 12. Set level 1 enemy ticks-between-attacks to 12. Scale enemy ticks-between-attacks down linearly so that a level 100 enemy attacks every 2 ticks. Use the formula: enemyTicks = round(12 - (10 / 99) * (level - 1)), clamped to a minimum of 2. Expose both the player tick rate and each monster's tick rate as visible constants/derivations so future gear-based reductions can be applied as modifiers. Update CharacterSheet.tsx and DungeonRunScreen.tsx to reflect the new base values.

In lootGenerator.ts, scale down the average value of all randomly generated item stat affixes by 75% across all item types (Weapon, Armor, Trinket/Charm) and all rarity tiers. This applies to base stat values (physical damage on weapons, defense on armor) and all affix rolls (Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance). Reduce the midpoint/average of each random range by 75% while preserving the relative spread between min and max.

Replace DungeonSelectScreen.tsx's Catacombs and Depths mode selector with a level-input combat screen. The player enters a monster level between 1 and their current character level (inclusive). The monster is scaled to that level (HP, damage, defense, tick interval, loot quality, XP reward all scale proportionally). The Ascension Trial remains accessible from this screen as a distinct button or section, separate from the level-input flow. Remove the Catacombs and Depths mode logic from the dungeon entry path; Ascension Trial mode logic is preserved.

In combatEngine.ts and/or lootGenerator.ts, implement an XP and drop rate penalty when the monster level chosen by the player is more than 5 levels below the player's current character level. When this condition is met (playerLevel - monsterLevel > 5), apply a 50% reduction to XP earned and a 50% reduction to the item drop rate for that run. Log or annotate the penalty in the combat result so the frontend can display it to the player.

In DungeonSelectScreen.tsx and combatEngine.ts, enforce a maximum fightable monster level of the player's current character level + 5. If the player types a value higher than (character level + 5) in the monster level input field, display an inline error message stating the level is not possible (e.g. 'Maximum monster level is [playerLevel + 5]'). Prevent starting a dungeon run while an out-of-range value is entered. The lower bound remains 1.

In useLocalCharacter.ts and/or a new useHealthSync hook, after every dungeon run completes persist the character's post-combat HP to the backend via setCharacterHp. On app load, read currentHp from the backend character record and initialize local HP state to that value rather than maxHp. This ensures damage taken in a previous session carries into the next session.

In combatEngine.ts, accept the character's current HP as input to the combat simulation rather than always computing HP from base stats alone. The player enters each fight with their actual current HP. If currentHp < maxHp at fight start, the combat engine uses the lower value as the starting HP. On fight completion, the remaining HP is returned in the combat result for persistence.

Add a persistent live health bar component displayed at the top of every screen (inside Navigation.tsx or as a sibling element rendered from App.tsx). It must show current HP / max HP numerically and as a visual bar. The bar must update in real time during the +2 HP/sec regen animation and after combat.

Outside of active combat, regenerate the player's current HP at +2 HP per second up to maxHp. Implement this as a client-side interval in App.tsx or useLocalCharacter.ts. The regen timer must produce a visible tick-up effect on the health bar every second. When HP reaches maxHp, the regen interval pauses. Regen does not run during an active dungeon run. After each regen tick, persist the updated HP to the backend via setCharacterHp so regen progress survives a page reload.

Replace the current realm-select / single-character flow in App.tsx with a character select screen shown immediately after login. The character select screen must list all of the player's existing characters (showing name, realm, class tier, level, and alive/dead status). It must include a 'Create New Character' button (disabled with a tooltip when the player already has 8 characters) and a 'Delete' button per character slot (with a confirmation prompt before deletion). Selecting a character routes the player to the character sheet / dungeon select screen for that character.

Update all frontend queries and mutations in useQueries.ts to support multi-character operations: fetching all characters for the current principal, creating a character (with #limitReached error handling), and deleting a character by ID. The active character ID must be tracked in App.tsx state so all screens reference the correct character when multiple exist.

When a new character is created, automatically grant them a starter equipment set consisting of at minimum one Weapon and one Armor piece. The starter weapon must provide enough base physical damage so that the character deals meaningful damage to level 1 monsters (well above 1 damage). The starter armor must provide enough base defense to meaningfully reduce incoming damage from level 1 monsters (which currently deal ~13 damage). Generate these items using the existing lootGenerator.ts item generation logic at item level 1 with Common rarity, then immediately equip them to the character's weapon and armor slots. This must happen at character creation time so the player never starts a dungeon run unequipped.

In DungeonSelectScreen.tsx, remove the non-functional drop rate percentage scroller/slider UI element. Replace it with a clean static informational display showing the current effective XP rate and drop rate for the selected monster level, calculated using the existing flat 6% base drop rate logic (6% base, reduced by 1% per level the monster is below the player's level, flooring at 0%). The display should be read-only text, matching the cleaner informational style used in prior versions of the screen.

Fix stat allocation persistence during character creation: when the player distributes their 8 starting stat points and confirms creation in CharacterCreation.tsx, the allocated Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality values must be passed directly to the createCharacter backend call and written into the character's baseStats record in stable storage. No post-creation step may overwrite these values with defaults. On the character sheet, all four stats must reflect the player-chosen values immediately after creation.

Fix starter equipment not being equipped after character creation: trace the full creation flow in CharacterCreation.tsx and useLocalCharacter.ts to identify why the generated starter Weapon and Armor items are not appearing in the character's equipment slots. Ensure that after createCharacter resolves, the starter weapon is placed in the weapon equipment slot and the starter armor is placed in the armor equipment slot in both local state and persisted storage before routing to the character sheet.

Add a Delete Character button to the character creation flow: on CharacterCreation.tsx, when the player navigates to create a new character, display a list of their existing characters (if any) with a Delete button next to each. Clicking Delete must show a confirmation prompt before permanently removing the character via the deleteCharacter backend call. This is in addition to the existing Delete button on CharacterSelectScreen.tsx — both entry points must support deletion.

Fix the ability points mismatch: ensure the available ability point count displayed on the character summary (CharacterSheet.tsx) and the count shown inside the AbilitySelectModal.tsx both derive from the same single source of truth using the formula: floor((level - 1) / 10) + 1, so that a level 1 character always shows exactly 1 available ability point in both locations. The abilities screen must never show 0 when the character summary shows 1.

Fix the character select screen HP display: each character card in CharacterSelectScreen.tsx must display the character's correct maxHp (derived from base stats + Vitality bonus, using the same formula as combatEngine.ts and CharacterSheet.tsx) rather than the hardcoded or default value of 20. The currentHp displayed on the card must also reflect the persisted currentHp value read from the backend character record.

In useQueries.ts, ensure the getCharacters query (or equivalent character list fetch) has a defined timeout and does not hang indefinitely. If the backend call fails, rejects, or does not resolve within a reasonable window, the query must settle into an error state rather than remaining in a perpetual loading state. Configure React Query's retry count to at most 1 retry for this query to prevent long hang times.

In App.tsx, audit the top-level loading state logic that gates rendering after login. Ensure the loading flag is explicitly set to false on both the success and error paths of the character fetch. If the character fetch errors or returns an empty result, the app must render the character select screen (empty state) rather than staying on a loading screen. No code path may leave the app in a permanent loading state.

In App.tsx or CharacterSelectScreen.tsx, add an error boundary / fallback for the character list query. If `isError` is true on the characters query, display a user-facing error message (e.g., 'Could not load characters. Please refresh the page.') with a retry button that re-triggers the query, rather than leaving the user on a blank loading screen.

In the frontend combat result submission flow (App.tsx, DungeonRunScreen.tsx, or useQueries.ts — whichever calls the backend after a dungeon run), ensure the earned XP and resulting level from the CombatResult are passed to the backend submitDungeonResult (or equivalent) call after every completed run, including both victories and Softcore deaths. After the backend call resolves, invalidate or refetch the active character query so CharacterSheet.tsx and XPBar.tsx immediately reflect the persisted XP and level.

In combatEngine.ts, after the combat simulation completes (win, loss, or Softcore death), include the player's remaining HP in the returned CombatResult object. This value must be the actual HP remaining at the end of combat — 0 on death for Softcore. On a Softcore death the returned HP should be a small positive value (e.g. 1) rather than 0, so the player can regen back to full.

In DungeonRunScreen.tsx, after the dungeon run completes and the backend submission resolves, call the setCharacterHp backend mutation with the remainingHp value from the CombatResult. Then update the local HP state in App.tsx (or useLocalCharacter.ts) to that same remainingHp value so the health bar and regen interval immediately start from the correct post-combat HP rather than defaulting back to 20.

In useHealthRegen.ts (or wherever the regen interval is initialized), ensure the regen interval reads its starting HP from the backend-persisted currentHp value each time a character is selected or the app loads. It must not hardcode or default to 20. If currentHp equals maxHp, the regen interval must not fire.

Fix stat point grants on level-up. In types/game.ts or wherever unspentStatPoints is calculated, ensure the formula correctly accounts for all levels gained. The unspentStatPoints for a character should be: (currentLevel - 1) minus the total number of stat points already spent. On CharacterSheet.tsx, when LevelUpModal is triggered, the number of allocatable points must equal the newly earned stat points (1 per level gained). After the player allocates and confirms in LevelUpModal.tsx, persist the updated baseStats to the backend via the appropriate mutation so the new stat values survive a page reload.

In DungeonRunScreen.tsx and combatEngine.ts, fix all combat log message grammar: replace every occurrence of 'you attacks' with 'you attack' and every occurrence of 'you uses' with 'you use'. Audit all combat log string templates for any other second-person verb conjugation errors and fix them to use the base verb form.

In DungeonRunScreen.tsx, remove the artificial ~2 second delay that occurs before combat begins. Combat simulation must start rendering immediately with no loading pause or loading spinner before the first combat tick is displayed.

In DungeonRunScreen.tsx, remove the post-combat 'saving' pause and any UI element that informs the player that results are being saved (e.g. 'Saving...', 'Submitting results...' messages or spinners). Backend persistence must happen silently and immediately after combat ends without blocking the player from seeing results or navigating away.

In monsters.ts, set the base HP of all level 1 monster templates to approximately 30 HP. Review and fix the HP scaling formula in monsters.ts and combatEngine.ts so that monster HP scales sensibly from ~30 at level 1 upward, avoiding the current situation where level 1 monsters have hundreds of HP. Scaling should produce a smooth, meaningful progression curve (e.g. roughly doubling or tripling HP by level 10, not jumping to 300+ at level 1).

Fix HP reset when returning to the character select screen: when the player backs out to CharacterSelectScreen.tsx and then re-selects a character, the local HP state and regen interval must initialize from the backend-persisted currentHp value for that character — not from maxHp or any hardcoded default. The persisted currentHp is the single source of truth. No transition, navigation event, or character re-selection may overwrite the current HP with maxHp unless the character's backend record already stores maxHp as the currentHp value.

Rework the enemy tick rate formula in combatEngine.ts and monsters.ts: set the player's base ticks-between-attacks to 12 (unchanged). Set level 1 enemy ticks-between-attacks to 21. Scale enemy ticks down by 1 for every 5 levels gained (level 1 = 21, level 6 = 20, level 11 = 19, level 16 = 18, … continuing down), with a minimum floor of 2 ticks. Replace the previous linear interpolation formula entirely with this new step-based formula. Update CharacterSheet.tsx and DungeonRunScreen.tsx to display the corrected values.

Scale XP rewards in combatEngine.ts based on both the monster's level AND the monster's generated stats. Compute a stat-power multiplier from the monster's actual HP, attack, and defense values relative to the baseline for that level. Monsters whose generated stats significantly exceed the baseline (e.g. elder gods or dragons with high defense and HP) must yield proportionally more XP than a weak low-stat monster at the same level. The base level-based XP value is multiplied by this stat-power factor before being returned in the CombatResult. The multiplier must never reduce XP below the level-based base (floor at 1.0×).

In CharacterSelectScreen.tsx, audit the 'Enter The Realm' (or equivalent character select / play) button's onClick handler. Identify why it produces zero feedback and does not navigate when clicked. Ensure the handler correctly invokes the character selection callback (e.g. onSelectCharacter or equivalent prop passed from App.tsx), passing the selected character's ID. The button must not be silently disabled, have a missing or undefined handler, or have its event swallowed by an outer element.

In App.tsx, verify that the onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent) passed down to CharacterSelectScreen is correctly defined and sets the active character ID in App-level state, then transitions the routing state to the character sheet / dungeon select screen. Ensure no intermediate loading flag or guard condition silently blocks the transition after the callback fires.

In CharacterSelectScreen.tsx, audit the 'Enter The Realm' button's onClick handler end-to-end. Identify and fix every reason the button produces zero feedback and does not navigate when clicked. Specifically: (1) confirm the button is not conditionally disabled or rendered without an onClick; (2) confirm the handler calls the onSelectCharacter (or equivalent) prop with the selected character's ID; (3) confirm no outer element is swallowing the click event (e.g. a pointer-events-none overlay or a stopPropagation call); (4) confirm the selected character state is non-null at the time the button is clicked so the ID passed is valid; (5) remove any guard or early-return that silently prevents the callback from firing.

In App.tsx, audit the onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent) that is passed as a prop to CharacterSelectScreen. Verify: (1) the callback is defined as a stable function (useCallback or equivalent) and is not undefined at render time; (2) it correctly writes the selected character's ID into App-level state (e.g. activeCharacterId); (3) after setting the ID it immediately updates the routing state to transition to the character sheet / dungeon select screen; (4) no intermediate loading flag, isLoading guard, or conditional render silently blocks the screen transition after the callback fires; (5) if any such guard exists, ensure it evaluates to false (i.e. not blocking) by the time the callback completes.

new_feature_requests.summary: Shared persistent on-chain world with Internet Identity auth (Motoko backend)
Real-time tick-based combat (client-side, server-validated on completion) with 10 monster types
Character progression: leveling, 4 core stats (Strength/Dexterity/Intelligence/Vitality), stat allocation
8-12 abilities across melee/ranged/magic/tank styles, max 3 equipped, reset on MQ/reroll
Loot system: randomized affixes, 4 rarity tiers, inventory with item compare tooltips
Ascension Trial (Master Quest) encounter gating class tier unlocks; auto season reset on final MQ completion
ICP-based marketplace: list, bid, buy items; cross-player item transfers and item vault/stash
Live leaderboard with Softcore and Hardcore realms; Hardcore characters persist on ladder after death; full wipe on season reset
3+ dungeon modes: Catacombs, Depths (high risk/reward), Ascension Trial dungeon
All UI screens: character sheet, dungeon run, inventory, ladder, MQ/Ascension Trial, marketplace
Professions and shrines as stubbed placeholders for future expansion
Softcore death penalty (gold/progress loss) and Hardcore death (character deleted, stash retained across seasons)

Implement the Motoko backend actor with persistent on-chain storage for: players (principal → character data), characters (stats, class tier, level, XP, realm, alive/dead status), inventory, stash/vault, season number, ladder rankings, and marketplace listings.

Implement the Ascension Trial (Master Quest) system. When a character meets the milestone requirement (level/XP threshold) for their current class tier, they may attempt the Ascension Trial. Completing it unlocks the next class tier. When a player completes the final class tier's Ascension Trial, trigger an automatic global season reset: all characters revert to Class Tier 1 / Level 1; stash and meta progression are retained; the ladder wipes.
Fix equipment stats not applied to character: weapon base physical damage, armor base defense, and all affixes summed into effective stats for combat and character sheet
Monster name variety: prefix/suffix name generation system across all 10 monster types
Ticks Between Attacks as a visible stat on character sheet and combat UI for both player and monsters
Fix routing: after login, if character already exists for selected realm, skip character creation and route directly to character sheet/dungeon screen (applies to both Softcore and Hardcore)

Replace dungeon mode selector (Catacombs/Depths) with level-input combat: player types monster level (1-100), enemy scales accordingly, XP and loot scale with level. Ascension Trial remains separately accessible. Fighting enemies 5+ levels below player applies 50% XP and 50% drop rate penalty.

Fix the post-login routing logic in App.tsx so that if a character already exists for the selected realm (Softcore or Hardcore), the app skips character creation entirely and routes the player directly to the character sheet / dungeon screen. This must apply to both Softcore and Hardcore realms. The character creation screen should only appear when no character exists for the chosen realm in the current season.

Replace the dungeon mode selector screen (DungeonSelectScreen.tsx) that offers Catacombs and Depths modes with a level-input combat screen. The player types in a monster level between 1 and 100 and initiates a dungeon run against a single monster scaled to that level. Difficulty (monster HP, damage, defense), XP rewards, and loot quality must all scale proportionally with the chosen monster level. The Ascension Trial must remain accessible separately from this screen (e.g., as a distinct button or tab) and must not be replaced.

In the backend (main.mo), ensure the createCharacter function returns a clear #alreadyExists error variant when a character already exists for the calling principal in the selected realm and current season. The frontend must never receive an ambiguous error for this case.

Ability cycling in combat: each attack tick rolls a proc — 87.5% normal attack / 12.5% ability with 1 equipped, 75% / 12.5% each with 2, 62.5% / 12.5% each with 3. When ability procs, it fully replaces the normal attack with that ability's defined effect.

Tick scaling: player base 12 ticks between attacks; level 1 enemies 12 ticks; level 100 enemies 2 ticks; smooth linear interpolation across levels 1-100. Leave headroom for future gear-based reductions.
Scale down random loot stat averages by 75% across all generated items in lootGenerator.ts.
Drop rate rework: flat 6% base drop rate, -1% per level the monster is below player's level, hitting 0% at 6+ levels below. Replaces the old 50% penalty system.
Monster level cap: max fightable level = player's current level + 5. Typing a higher value shows an error message saying it isn't possible.

In lootGenerator.ts, replace the existing XP/drop rate penalty system entirely with a new flat drop rate system: base drop rate is 6%. For each level the monster is below the player's current character level, reduce the drop rate by 1% (so a monster 1 level below = 5%, 2 below = 4%, ... 6 or more below = 0%). Remove the old logic that applied a 50% drop rate cut for monsters 5+ levels below the player. This new system applies to all dungeon modes and all monster types.
Persistent live health bar: displayed at top of every screen, damage carries over between fights, +2 HP/sec regen outside combat with visible animation, HP stored on-chain to survive page refreshes
Multi-character system: up to 8 characters per account (any mix of Softcore/Hardcore), character select screen after login, create character button (capped at 8), delete character option

In backend/main.mo, add a currentHp field to the character record. Persist the character's current HP on-chain so it survives page refreshes and browser closes. Expose a setCharacterHp(characterId, hp: Nat) update function callable by the authenticated principal (owner only). The currentHp value is initialized to the character's maximum HP on creation and must never exceed maxHp.

In backend/main.mo, remove the one-character-per-realm constraint. Replace the per-principal character storage with a structure that allows up to 8 characters per principal across any mix of Softcore and Hardcore realms. Expose a deleteCharacter(characterId) update function callable only by the owning principal. Enforce the 8-character cap in createCharacter — return a #limitReached error variant if the caller already has 8 characters.
New characters receive starter equipment on creation (basic weapon + armor) to make early combat viable — fixes 1 damage output and low survivability at level 1
Remove drop % scroller from dungeon select screen; replace with static display of XP/drop rate info (flat 6% base, reduced by level gap)

Fix the persistent live health bar in Navigation.tsx (or wherever it is rendered) so that the max HP value it displays is derived using the same formula used by the combat engine and character sheet — base HP plus Vitality bonus contribution — rather than the hardcoded or default 20 HP it currently shows. The health bar's currentHp and maxHp props must both be computed from the character's effective stats (including Vitality) so all three displays (health bar, character sheet, combat engine) are in sync.
Fix ability points mismatch: character summary shows 1 available ability point at level 1 but abilities screen shows 0 — both must reflect the correct value (1 at level 1)
Fix character select screen showing wrong HP (20 instead of correct max HP derived from Vitality) and regen initializing from 20 instead of persisted currentHp

Fix the health regen initialization on character select: when the player selects a character and the app transitions to the game screens, initialize the local HP state and regen interval from the backend-persisted currentHp value for that character rather than from a hardcoded default of 20 or from maxHp. If currentHp is already at maxHp, the regen interval must not fire. If currentHp is below maxHp, regen must begin from the persisted value (e.g. if persisted currentHp is 45 and maxHp is 60, regen starts at 45 and ticks up — not from 20).
Fix XP not persisting after dungeon runs: after a run completes, earned XP and any level-ups must be saved to the backend character record in main.mo so progress carries over between sessions

In backend/main.mo, update the dungeon result submission handler (e.g. submitDungeonResult or equivalent) to write the updated XP and level values into the character's stable storage record after a completed run. The character's xp and level fields must reflect the combat result's earned XP and any level-ups triggered before the function returns. If no such submission function exists, add one that accepts the combat result (characterId, xpEarned, newLevel) from the authenticated principal and atomically updates the character record.
Fix HP not carrying over after battles: post-combat HP must persist to backend and regen must resume from that value instead of resetting to 20
Fix stat point not granted on level-up: level-up logic must correctly calculate and award unspent stat points, making them available on the character sheet

In backend/main.mo, ensure the submitDungeonResult (or equivalent) handler updates the character's statPoints field to reflect newly earned stat points from the run's level-up(s). Each level gained awards 1 stat point. The backend record must store both the total stat points earned and the total stat points spent, so unspent = earned - spent can always be computed correctly on the frontend.
Fix combat log grammar: 'you attacks' → 'you attack' and 'you uses' → 'you use'
Remove pre-combat loading delay (instant start) and post-combat save delay + saving notification (silent immediate persistence)
Rebalance monster HP: set level 1 base HP to ~30, with sensible scaling upward from there
Fix HP reset when returning to character select screen: persisted currentHp must be preserved when backing out to character select and re-selecting — not reset to max HP

Fix player attack tick rate: player base = 12 ticks. Level 1 enemies = 21 ticks, scaling down by 1 tick every 5 levels (level 6=20, level 11=19, etc.), floor of 2 ticks. Replaces previous enemy tick formula.

Scale XP rewards based on both monster level AND monster generated stats (HP, defense, attack). Higher-stat monsters (e.g. elder gods, dragons with high defense) yield more XP than weak low-stat monsters at the same level.
